# UC-23.6.3 · Budget vs Actual Variance Tracking

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We compare what each department planned to spend against what really posted each month so managers see meaningful overshoots early—before leadership asks why numbers shifted during the formal close meeting.*

---

## Description

Joins ERP-native annual operating plan and quarterly rolling forecast amounts ingested from universal-journal plan tables (or equivalent budget extracts) with posted actual general-ledger activity at company-code × cost-centre × account × fiscal-period grain, applies FP&A-style favourable versus unfavourable rules by account type, and flags rows breaching dual materiality tests so FP&A can steer preliminary P&L reviews around Day five through seven close milestones instead of discovering drift only after static board packs freeze.

## Value

Department heads receive scored variance commentary tied to ownership metadata in lookups before leadership asks for written explanations; the CFO office gains rolling drill paths into overspending centres that undermine rolling eighteen-month forecasts or driver-linked plans without confusing this signal with customer-side collections from accounts-receivable aging or reimbursement anomaly spikes from travel-and-entertainment programmes. Outputs anchor discipline scorecards and board-deck narratives while acknowledging consolidation lag and FX-only swings called out during reconciliation.

## Implementation

Stand up DB Connect rising or batch inputs projecting `ACDOCA` actual rows (`VERSN` actuals) and `ACDOCP` plan rows (`VERSN` AOP plus quarterly forecasts), publish `cost_centre_master.csv` and `gl_account_master.csv` under Splunk lookups with finance-approved materiality floors by entity, schedule this SPL daily once intraday postings settle (avoid Day one noise before allocations), clone panels for Oracle `GL_BALANCES` versus `GL_BUDGETS` sourcetypes where SAP is absent, route Critical escalate rows to FP&A Slack with throttle by `RBUKRS`,`RCNTR`,`RACCT`, and archive weekly CSV snapshots for audit trails.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier; Heavy Forwarder or containerized worker with DB Connect licensed and network reach to read-only SAP HANA, Oracle EBS reporting views, or a certified finance warehouse snapshot that finance leadership signs as month-to-date authoritative for management reporting.
- Database roles limited to curated views exposing `ACDOCA`, `ACDOCP`, and `CSKS` (or Oracle `GL_BALANCES` / `GL_BUDGETS` joins) with predicates that exclude statistical and sample clients your policy never books to external statements.
- Lookup files `cost_centre_master.csv` and `gl_account_master.csv` governed by controlling; include `materiality_floor_usd` per `RBUKRS` or `RCNTR` so public-company significance policy (often five hundred thousand dollars SOX gates) can diverge from ten thousand dollar operational coaching thresholds.
- RBAC separating operational FP&A editors from consolidated reporting teams because variance queues occasionally preview unreleased forecasts.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install Splunk DB Connect from Splunkbase 2686; register JDBC drivers (`ngdbc.jar`, `ojdbc*.jar`). Build connections whose identities cannot mutate master data.
For SAP, schedule batched rising queries wrapping `SELECT` projections from `ACDOCA` where ledger postings reflect closed operational activity (`VERSN` zero padded actuals) and parallel pulls from `ACDOCP` filtered to planning ledger variants (`VERSN` hundred block plus quarterly forecasts one-o-one through one-o-four when KP06/KP46/KP56 planners publish those slices). Stamp `_time` from ERP extraction timestamps rather than indexer receipt when CFO cadence compares flash timing.
Emit sourcetypes exactly named `dbx:sap_acdoca` and `dbx:sap_acdocp` into `index=business`; duplicate structural columns (`RBUKRS`,`RCNTR`,`RACCT`,`GJAHR`,`POPER`,`HSL`,`VERSN`,`VRGNG`,`BSTAT`) so SPL macros remain portable.
Stand optional modular HTTPS collectors hitting Oracle `/planning/rest/v3/applications/{app}/plantypes/{plan_type}`, Adaptive `api.adaptivecloud.com`, Anaplan `/2/0/workspaces/{ws_id}/models/{model_id}/views/{view_id}/data`, or OneStream `/api/Application/{app_id}/Cube/{cube_name}/Data`; normalize outputs into interim staging indexes until field parity matches JDBC feeds.
Upload lookups via Lookup Editor or app bundles and restrict write ACLs to controller stewards.

### Step 2 — Saved search and thresholds
Promote the SPL in this JSON into a macro-parameterized saved search (`finance_variance_material`) where earliest/latest macros reflect fiscal calendars (`macro-finance-period-start`). Extend dual thresholds (`materiality_floor`, `variance_pct_gate`) via lookup-driven integers rather than literals embedded throughout SPL tokens.
Attach alerts only on statuses containing escalate or investigate with suppression keys `RBUKRS`+`RCNTR`+`RACCT` twelve hours to survive repeated DB Connect replay.
Clone a second saved search swapping sourcetypes for Oracle `dbx:ebs_gl_actuals` / `dbx:ebs_gl_budgets`, mapping `CODE_COMBINATION_ID` splits into `RCNTR`/`RACCT` analogues prior to identical variance math.

### Step 3 — Validate against ERP postings
Pick three material rows Splunk surfaces; for each tuple open SAP transaction FAGLL03 (or CE-GL line reporting) validating `sum(HSL)` for the same fiscal year, period, account, cost object, and company code matches within rounding tolerance versus the JDBC extract.
Validate plan slices by comparing `ACDOCP` sums to KE30 or costing-plan reports for identical `VERSN` and version descriptions your team labels AOP versus Q-two forecast.
Oracle shops tie Splunk aggregates to Oracle BI publisher GL trial reports plus Hyperion freeze dashboards.
Document FX alignment: confirm variance compares local amounts consistently or converts both legs using treasury-published monthly-average rates before declaring FX-driven exceptions.

### Step 4 — Dashboards and cadence
Publish Dashboard Studio workbook anchored on finance close rhythm: annotate panels when preliminary P&L briefings occur Day five versus final Day seven locks.
Embed drill-through links into ERP Fiori apps using encoded URL macros carrying document numbers (`RBELN`) via secondary drill searches stored alongside main panels.
Export weekly PNG snapshots into board collaboration folders feeding CFO commentary drafts scheduled Day ten versus Day fifteen pre-read milestones leadership expects.
Feed discipline scorecards by joining Splunk CSV outputs into HR-owned departmental KPI portals via scripted nightly pushes.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- Allocation timing — KSU5/KSV5 cycles frequently post Day three of close; Day one variance treats pre-allocation postings as standalone overspend.
- CO-PA reconciliation lag — CE1xxxx profitability lines sometimes trail universal journal revenue by twenty-four hours; Day one pairing misses aligned COGS causing faux favourable revenue variance.
- Foreign currency translation timing — OB08 monthly rates publishing late separates plan-USD legs from actual-USD legs despite flat operations; recompute under same `TCURR` type.
- Plan-version drift — Comparing actuals strictly to `VERSN` one hundred annual operating plan ignores current-quarter `101`–`104` rolling forecast your CEO already briefed—align Splunk macro to the version string leadership references in steering meetings.
- Mid-year cost-centre reorganisation — CSKS hierarchy changes via KS01/KS02 do not rewrite prior actuals into new centres; merged `RCNTR` codes may show phantom overspend until mapping tables allocate history.
- Accrual reversal timing — FBS1-style accruals reversing on first day of next period leave a one-day vacuum where variance shows missing accrual offset until replacement posts land.
- Statistical key figures — Missing KSEN/KSEU driver loads stall distribution cycles dependent on SKF percentages, cascading false materiality in dependent accounts.
- Reporting hierarchy mismatch — Report Painter set definitions grouping EMEA manufacturing nodes may not match Splunk lookup rollups named `EMEA-MFG-001`.
- Top-side adjustments — MEC1 special-period entries or consolidation-only journals may bypass subledger feeds yet move group P&amp;L Splunk excludes if SQL filters omit ledger variants.
- Discontinued operations — IFRS five / ASC two-o-five segregation demands filters on `gl_account_master.csv` DiscOps flags so variance commentary stays with continuing ops.
- One-time non-GAAP items — Restructuring or impairment hits flagged non-operating should bypass operational variance thresholds via CSV exclusions.
- Capex versus opex miscoding — Vendor invoices temporarily hitting expense accounts ahead of asset activation inflate variance until ANEK/ANEP tie clears.
- Accounts payable cut-off — FB60 postings dated last period while goods receipt landed next distort variance until reversing pairs settle.
- Plan refresh cadence — Annual `VERSN` one hundred barely moves while quarterly forecasts update monthly; stale comparisons misstate discipline versus narrative forecasts finance expects.
- Subsidiary consolidation timing — Regional closes arriving Day four versus holding-company Day two snapshots omit sizable revenue blocks until BPC loads finish.

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_acdoca" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
| where tostring(VERSN) IN ("000","00","0")
| where BSTAT!="A" OR isnull(BSTAT)
| eval period=tostring(GJAHR)."-".printf("%02d",tonumber(POPER))
| eval amount_lc=tonumber(HSL)
| stats sum(amount_lc) as actual_amount by RBUKRS, RCNTR, RACCT, period
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_acdocp" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | where VERSN IN ("100","101","102","103","104")
    | eval period=tostring(GJAHR)."-".printf("%02d",tonumber(POPER))
    | eval amount_lc=tonumber(HSL)
    | stats sum(amount_lc) as budget_amount by RBUKRS, RCNTR, RACCT, period, VERSN
    | stats sum(budget_amount) as budget_amount by RBUKRS, RCNTR, RACCT, period ]
| stats sum(actual_amount) as actual_amount, sum(budget_amount) as budget_amount by RBUKRS, RCNTR, RACCT, period
| lookup cost_centre_master.csv RCNTR OUTPUT cost_centre_name, dept, owner_email, materiality_floor_usd
| lookup gl_account_master.csv RACCT OUTPUT account_name, account_type, p_l_or_bs
| fillnull value=0 actual_amount budget_amount
| eval variance_lc=actual_amount-budget_amount
| eval variance_pct=if(budget_amount!=0, round(100*variance_lc/abs(budget_amount), 1), null())
| eval is_unfavourable=case(
    account_type IN ("EXP","COGS") AND variance_lc>0, 1,
    account_type IN ("REV") AND variance_lc<0, 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval materiality_floor=coalesce(materiality_floor_usd, 10000)
| eval is_material=if(abs(variance_lc)>=materiality_floor AND abs(variance_pct)>=5, 1, 0)
| eval status=case(
    is_material=0, "Below threshold",
    is_unfavourable=1 AND abs(variance_pct)>=20, "Critical escalate",
    is_unfavourable=1 AND abs(variance_pct)>=10, "Unfavourable investigate",
    is_unfavourable=1, "Unfavourable monitor",
    1=1, "Favourable")
| where is_material=1
| sort - variance_lc
| table RBUKRS, cost_centre_name, dept, owner_email, account_name, account_type, period, budget_amount, actual_amount, variance_lc, variance_pct, status
```

## Visualization

Variance heatmap (cost-centre × account-class bucketed from `account_type`). Bar chart of top ten unfavourable material variances by absolute `variance_lc`. Trend chart of rolling three-month sum of signed variance against zero reference. Single-value tiles: total unfavourable dollars (EXP/COGS positive variance plus REV negative variance), prior-period delta token, count of material rows breaching thresholds. Area chart of year-to-date cumulative actual versus plan per `dept` for chief executive storyline slides.

## Known False Positives

SAP allocation cycles tied to KSU5 or KSV5 often land around Day three of close; variance snapshots taken Day one or two attribute overhead before cycles finish so centres look artificially unfavourable until postings settle. Universal-journal extracts filtered only by period sometimes mix planning sandbox rows when DB Connect pulls reuse dev schemas — totals oscillate until SQL predicates exclude client two-two-two test ledgers. Frozen Oracle `GL_BUDGET_VERSIONS` marked open versus frozen confuse Splunk readers when interim uploads overlap frozen annual envelopes; dashboards spike until controllers publish final freeze timestamps. SaaS rolling forecast pulls (`VERSN` `101`–`104`) refreshed asynchronously versus nightly ERP polls produce phantom drift identical to FX noise until timestamps align on `_time`. Multi-currency portfolios comparing `HSL` variance against USD-only floors from lookup yield misleading breach counts though economic substance sits inside statutory translation buffers—mirror treasury rate-type policies (`TCURR` type `M` versus `E`) before reacting. Capital-project invoices parked temporarily on operating expense accounts ahead of asset capitalization (`ANEK`/`ANEP` not yet tied) inflate opex variance without operations truly overspending. Mid-year reorganizations that remap `RCNTR` through CSKS hierarchy without back-allocating prior months make merged centres resemble runaway spend versus true year-to-date continuity. Reverse-audit top-side journals in special periods (`MEC1` thirteen through sixteen equivalents) absent from operational subledgers swing variance for one day only. Driver-based forecast lines (headcount linked to bonus accruals) may show favourability simply because payroll accruals reverse on a different business day than planned, not because talent costs structurally declined.
